text:
**1. Today (the home)**
The default and 90% of usage. A vertical list of today's exercises, each showing prescribed targets as ghosted placeholders. You tap to log actual. The whole thing is a big fillable form that reads like: [...] Logging a set is one or two taps — the weight/reps pre-fill from the plan or your last session, you nudge them if reality differed. A set is either "as planned" (one tap) or "adjusted" (tap the number, dial it). Skipping, substituting, adding a set, flagging pain/fatigue are all one gesture away, never buried in a menu. The distinction between *prescribed* (ghost) and *actual* (solid) is always visible.
